description: A comprehensive training program for building autonomous drone systems that monitor crop health, soil conditions, pest infestations, irrigation efficiency, and environmental parameters (wildlife, deforestation, coastal erosion). The program covers all four engineering tracks—AI, MLOps, Data Engineering, and Edge AI—applied specifically to agriculture and environmental stewardship, enabling precision farming, yield prediction, and ecological monitoring.
learning_objectives: Understand agricultural and environmental monitoring requirements (crop types, phenology, pest cycles, regulatory reporting)
Build and train AI models for plant health assessment (NDVI, disease detection, pest identification), yield estimation, and wildlife counting
Create geospatial and multispectral data pipelines for field‑scale analysis and long‑term trend monitoring
Deploy optimized models on edge hardware for real‑time field scouting and targeted treatment
Implement automated reporting and integration with farm management software (e.g., Climate FieldView, John Deere Operations Center)
Design efficient flight paths for large‑area coverage with battery constraints
Comply with agricultural aviation regulations and environmental reporting standards

modules.title: Module 1: Precision Agriculture and Environmental Monitoring Fundamentals

lessons.title: Crop Science and Environmental Indicators
lessons.content: Study key crops (corn, soy, wheat, rice, vineyards, orchards) and their growth stages (planting, emergence, flowering, grain fill, maturity). Learn about stress factors: water deficit, nutrient deficiency (N, P, K), disease (fungal, bacterial), pest damage (insects, rodents). Understand vegetation indices: NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index), NDRE (Red Edge), PRI (Photochemical Reflectance Index). For environmental monitoring: deforestation tracking, wildlife population counts, coastal erosion measurement, water quality assessment (turbidity, algae blooms).

lessons.title: Operational Scenarios and Requirements
lessons.content: Analyze scenarios: crop scouting (weekly flights to detect early stress), variable rate application (spraying only affected zones), yield prediction (before harvest), wildlife census (counting migratory birds or elephants), deforestation patrol (detecting illegal logging), coastal erosion mapping (repeat flights over shorelines). Define requirements: multispectral sensors (RedEdge, Altum), long flight endurance (30+ min), RTK GPS for precise geolocation, cloud‑based analytics, integration with farm machinery (tractors, sprayers), and regulatory compliance for pesticide application.

modules.title: Module 2: AI Engineer – Plant Health, Pest Detection, and Wildlife Monitoring

lessons.title: Vegetation Index Calculation and Health Mapping
lessons.content: Implement algorithms to compute NDVI, NDRE, and other indices from multispectral images. Use these indices to create health maps where high values indicate healthy vegetation, low values indicate stress. Train a classification model (e.g., Random Forest, small CNN) to categorize zones: healthy, moderate stress, severe stress. Output prescription maps for variable rate application.

lessons.title: Disease and Pest Detection
lessons.content: Train a detection model (e.g., YOLOv8, Faster R‑CNN) to identify disease lesions (rust, blight) and pest insects (aphids, beetles) from high‑resolution RGB or multispectral imagery. For diseases, use spectral signatures (e.g., specific reflectance bands). For insects, use object detection on images taken at low altitude. Build a dataset of labeled images from agricultural extension services.
lessons.code_example:
from ultralytics import YOLO
model = YOLO('yolov8n.pt')
model.train(data='pest_disease_dataset.yaml', epochs=100)

lessons.title: Yield Estimation and Fruit Counting
lessons.content: Use object counting models to estimate yield: detect and count fruits (apples, grapes, oranges) or corn ears. Train a detection model on high‑resolution imagery; for dense clusters, use density estimation (e.g., CSRNet). Calibrate counts with ground truth to predict total yield per hectare. For grains, use regression models on canopy cover and height (from LiDAR or stereo).
lessons.code_example:
import torch
from csrnet import CSRNet
model = CSRNet()
# Train on images with density maps

lessons.title: Wildlife Counting and Species Classification
lessons.content: Build a model to detect and count animals from aerial imagery (elephants, birds, marine life). Use object detection (YOLO) for larger animals, or segmentation for groups. For species classification, use a classifier on cropped detections. Handle challenges: animals partially occluded by vegetation, varying scales, and low contrast with background. Integrate with conservation databases.

modules.title: Module 3: Data Engineer – Field‑Scale Data Management

lessons.title: Geospatial Field Boundaries with PostGIS
lessons.content: Store field boundaries and crop information in PostGIS. Each field has geometry, crop type, planting date, expected harvest date, and owner. Use spatial queries to retrieve fields by location, crop, or inspection date. Store all flight data with reference to field boundaries.

lessons.title: STAC for Multispectral Data
lessons.content: Catalog every flight using STAC. Each item includes the flight’s bounding box, datetime, sensor (e.g., RedEdge), and computed NDVI map. Provide a STAC API for farmers, agronomists, and researchers to search by field, date, or vegetation index range.

lessons.title: Time‑Series Analysis for Crop Health Trends
lessons.content: Use TimescaleDB to store time‑series of NDVI, pest counts, etc., per field. Run analytics to detect trends (e.g., deteriorating health), compare with weather data, and predict optimal harvest windows. Build dashboards for farmers to visualize field health over the season.

modules.title: Module 4: Edge AI Engineer – Real‑Time Field Scouting

lessons.title: Hardware Configuration for Agricultural Drones
lessons.content: Select sensors: multispectral (e.g., MicaSense RedEdge, DJI P4 Multispectral), high‑resolution RGB (e.g., 20 MP), optionally thermal for irrigation stress detection. Use a drone with RTK GPS for accurate georeferencing. Connect to Jetson Orin Nano for on‑board computation of vegetation indices and real‑time anomaly detection. For spraying, use a drone with a tank and flow control.

lessons.title: DeepStream Pipeline for On‑the‑Fly NDVI
lessons.content: Build a DeepStream pipeline that ingests multispectral streams (e.g., five bands), computes NDVI or NDRE in real time using CUDA, and overlays a false‑color map on the video feed to the ground station. This allows the pilot to see stress areas instantly and adjust the flight plan.

lessons.title: ROS 2 Integration for Precision Spraying
lessons.content: Create a ROS 2 node that subscribes to NDVI maps and pest detections. When the drone is over a stressed zone, it commands the sprayer to activate, adjusting flow rate based on severity. Use MAVROS for waypoint following and payload control. Log all spraying actions for regulatory compliance.

modules.title: Module 5: ML Platform Engineer – Continuous Model Improvement

lessons.title: Active Learning for Rare Pests and Diseases
lessons.content: After each flight, collect images where pest/disease detection confidence is low or where the farmer reports a missed infestation. Send these to agronomists for labeling. Use active learning to retrain models periodically, improving detection of new or emerging threats.

lessons.title: Kubeflow Pipelines for Seasonal Retraining
lessons.content: Set up a Kubeflow pipeline that retrains models after each growing season, incorporating all new labeled data. Also retrain when new varieties or crops are introduced. Deploy updated models to the drone fleet before the next season.
lessons.code_example:
from kfp import dsl
@dsl.pipeline
def ag_model_retraining():
    data_prep = dsl.ContainerOp(...)
    train = dsl.ContainerOp(...).after(data_prep)
    evaluate = dsl.ContainerOp(...).after(train)
    deploy = dsl.ContainerOp(...).after(evaluate)

lessons.title: Monitoring Model Drift with Environmental Changes
lessons.content: Use Evidently AI to compare NDVI distributions and pest detection rates across years. Changes in climate, new crop varieties, or sensor upgrades can cause drift. Detect and trigger retraining to keep models accurate.

lessons.title: Hardware‑in‑the‑Loop (HIL) with Agricultural Scenes
lessons.content: Use NVIDIA Isaac Sim or Gazebo with realistic crop fields. Simulate plant growth, pest outbreaks, and disease spread. Connect real Pixhawk and Jetson. Test spraying missions, variable rate application, and wildlife counting. Validate that AI correctly identifies stressed zones and adjusts spray rates.
lessons.code_example: Isaac Sim spawns crop rows; drone flies grid; Jetson runs NDVI computation and commands sprayer.

lessons.title: Latency Profiling for Variable Rate Application
lessons.content: Profile the latency from NDVI computation to sprayer command. Use Nsight Systems to ensure the loop time is short enough for smooth application (e.g., < 200 ms). Optimize by using GPU‑accelerated indices and reducing image resolution where acceptable.

modules.title: Module 7: Safety, Regulatory, and Ethical Considerations

lessons.title: Agricultural Aviation Regulations
lessons.content: Study regulations for aerial application of pesticides (e.g., FAA Part 137 in US). Understand drift avoidance, buffer zones near water bodies, and record‑keeping requirements. Ensure that autonomous spray systems have manual override and geofencing to prevent off‑target application.

lessons.title: Environmental Stewardship
lessons.content: Avoid spraying during windy conditions. Use spot spraying to minimize chemical use. For environmental monitoring, respect wildlife and sensitive habitats (e.g., do not disturb nesting birds). Share data with conservation agencies to support sustainable practices.

lessons.title: Data Ownership and Privacy
lessons.content: Farmers own their field data. Implement clear data usage policies. Encrypt data in transit and storage. Provide options for farmers to control who can access their information.

capstone_project.name: Autonomous Crop Health Scouting and Targeted Spraying
capstone_project.description: Build and test a drone system that autonomously scouts a corn or soybean field, computes NDVI maps, identifies stressed zones (NDVI < 0.4), and performs targeted spraying on those zones. The system must log all flights and spray events, and generate a prescription map for future reference. Achieve >90% accuracy in identifying stressed zones and reduce chemical use by at least 30% compared to blanket spraying.
deliverables: ROS 2 nodes for flight planning, NDVI computation, and spray control
PostGIS/TimescaleDB database with field boundaries and inspection history
HIL simulation report showing spray efficiency

resources.name: UAV4Ag Dataset for Crop Disease Detection
